Patch Set 4: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 4:

(2 comments)

What exactly does "not well supported yet" mean?  What happens with group locks?

Patch Set 4:

(2 comments)

"What exactly does "not well supported yet" mean? What happens with group locks?"

I believe group locks will work as-is but I didn't do any tests. Do you mind to perform some tests if you happen to have available bandwidth?

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

(3 comments)

(defect) Since the original bug was so easy to reproduce we should add a test.

  
lfs setstripe -i 0 /mnt/lustre/f0
dd if=/dev/zero of=/mnt/lustre/f0 bs=1M &
sleep 1
cat /proc/fs/lustre/osc/lustre-OST0000-osc-ffff*/uuid > /proc/fs/lustre/obdfilter/lustre-OST0000/evict_client


Note that the group locked version (provided by Li Dongyang) now triggers a different LBUG().

parents.subject: LU-6215 ofed: strengthen ib_create_cq test in 4.2+ kernels

commit.subject: LU-6271 osc: handle osc eviction correctly
commit.message:
LU-6271 osc: handle osc eviction correctly

Cleanup everything if an OSC is being evicted.

Group lock is not well supported yet.

Signed-off-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
Change-Id: I24f815a9a1bf13cb565109b2dae7b4d07b57f921
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/14989
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Bobi Jam <bobijam@hotmail.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
